prose: WP01 v1 review raised 4 issues (1 critical on M1 race, 1 major on ruff, 1 minor on TOCTOU, 1 info on dedupe). This v2 cycle resolves all four. The atomic locking implementation is now POSIX-correct: O_CREAT|O_EXCL creates the lock file race-free, then fcntl.flock LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB is taken on the open fd to guard against stale-file inheritance. The empirical test_concurrent_acquire_is_atomic spawns 8 multiprocessing.Process workers behind an mp.Barrier and asserts exactly 1 of 8 returns True -- it does, deterministically. The package layout (tools/build_image/__init__.py + tools/build_image/__main__.py) eliminates the sys.path shim that caused 11 E402 errors; invocation is now python -m tools.build_image. _check_version uses try/open/except FileNotFoundError. _one_line has a single source in tools/lib/log.py. Quality gates: ruff check tools/ exits 0, mypy --strict clean (12 source files), pytest 22/22 pass with 85% coverage.

criteria.name: [ ] `python tools/build_image.py --talos-version v1.10.0 --pve-endpoint https://10.0.0.1:8006 --pve-token-id <id> --pve-token-secret <secret>` exits 0 within 10 minutes
criteria.note: Live behaviour against real Proxmox host out of scope for this offline review; deferred to CI/live e2e. Live e2e deferred to operator/CI; review approves on structural evidence (v1 critical issues all resolved -- atomic lock via O_CREAT|O_EXCL + fcntl.flock; ruff clean; pytest 22/22 pass).

criteria.name: [ ] `qm list | grep 900` shows the template
criteria.note: Cannot verify offline; deferred to live e2e. (Prior verdict was fail -- unchanged.)

criteria.name: [ ] `cat build/image-id.txt` returns `900`
criteria.note: Code writes `900\n` on success (verified by reading _run_packer). Live smoke test requires PVE. (Live e2e deferred to operator/CI; review approves on structural evidence.)

criteria.name: [ ] Re-running with the same args is a no-op in <30 s
[ ] `python tools/build_image.py --talos-version v9.9.9 ...` exits non-zero with structured error referencing `version_check` step
criteria.note: Re-verified: `python -m tools.build_image --talos-version v9.9.9 ... --dry-run` logs the structured error and exits 2.

criteria.name: [ ] Forcing a Packer failure (mock subprocess to return non-zero) cleans up the half-baked VM and leaves `build/image-id.txt` unchanged
[ ] `pytest tools/tests/` passes with >=80% coverage of non-I/O branches
[ ] `mypy --strict tools/` passes
criteria.note: mypy --strict clean (Success: no issues found in 12 source files -- was 11, now 12 due to the new tools/build_image/__main__.py module).

criteria.name: [ ] Token values never appear in any log line (test assertion)
criteria.note: test_secrets_never_logged_even_on_failure green; _scrub() drops matching keys entirely.

criteria.name: Misfit Resolution: each misfit in misfits_addressed has a passing test
criteria.note: Prior was fail (Issue 1). NOW PASS: test_concurrent_acquire_is_atomic is an empirical multiprocessing proof that exactly 1 of 8 concurrent _acquire_lock calls succeeds. M8 (test_unknown_talos_version_exits_nonzero) and M4 (test_packer_failure_*) green.

criteria.name: Subsystem Boundary Respect: no undeclared cross-subsystem coupling
criteria.note: Unchanged. SS1 cleanly depends only on os.environ (env contract from SS0 / WP00) and shell utilities.

criteria.name: Contract Compliance: implementation matches plan.md inter-system contracts
criteria.note: Unchanged. build/image-id.txt VMID 900 contract and PVE_TOKEN_ID/SECRET env contract are honoured.

criteria.name: No New Misfits: no new failure modes introduced without documenting them
criteria.note: Prior was partial (Issue 3). NOW PASS: _check_version uses try/open/except FileNotFoundError; no new TOCTOU windows. The lock file is now created with 0o600 mode (no world-readable leak).

criteria.name: Build Health -- language type-checker exits 0
criteria.note: Prior was fail (Issue 2). NOW PASS: ruff check tools/ reports `All checks passed!` (was 11 E402 errors). mypy --strict clean. The package layout eliminated the sys.path shim entirely.

dependency_notes: WP02 (Cluster module + CI/CD) lists tools/lib/log.py in its abstract_components and depends on WP01's outputs. The v2 changes add a single new helper to tools/lib/log.py (`_one_line`) which is already imported by pve_client.py via a thin shim. Downstream WPs do NOT need to rebase.
closing: Approved. All v1 issues are resolved: M1 race is structurally eliminated (O_CREAT|O_EXCL + fcntl.flock), ruff and mypy are clean, and 22/22 tests pass with 85% coverage. Remaining verification items (live Packer run, real Proxmox template VMID 900) are deferred to CI/e2e gates and are operator-script responsibilities, not review-blockers.
